description_full:
Given an integer array sorted in non-decreasing order, there is exactly one integer in the array that occurs more than 25% of the time, return that integer.

 
Example 1:


Input: arr = [1,2,2,6,6,6,6,7,10]
Output: 6


Example 2:


Input: arr = [1,1]
Output: 1


 
Constraints:


	1 <= arr.length <= 104
	0 <= arr[i] <= 105
description: Given an integer array sorted in non-decreasing order, there is exactly one integer in the array that occurs more than 25% of the time, return that integer.

subtasks.intent: Iterate through the sorted array, count consecutive occurrences of each number, and return the first one whose count exceeds 25% of the array's length.
